name: Stick
subtitle: Quick game · Conflict defender: flat defender

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The best quick concept in football because it works against every coverage and the read is one defender. Ball out in 1.5 seconds.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Slot runs a 5 to 6 yard hitch (the stick). A back or tight end runs the flat. The outside receiver runs a fade or go to clear space.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the flat defender (outside linebacker, nickel, or apex). IF he drops to cover the flat, throw the stick behind him. IF he sits on the stick, throw the flat. One defender, two receivers, someone is open.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3 and Cover 4 best (attacks the open flat). Solid against everything. Worst against heavy man with an underneath robber.

name: Slant-Flat
subtitle: Quick game · Conflict defender: flat defender

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The go-to quick man-beater. Ball out in 1.3 seconds, beats press, and is the immediate answer to a blitz.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Outside receiver runs a quick 3-step slant (break inside at 5 yards). Slot or back runs to the flat.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the flat defender. IF he widens to the flat, throw the slant inside. IF he sits on the slant, throw the flat.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3, Cover 1, and Cover 0 (throw it immediately, no safety help). Risky against Cover 2 (slant runs into the safety).

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The ultimate zone-beater. Five receivers divide the width of the field into horizontal windows. Three or four underneath defenders cannot cover five windows, so a void is guaranteed.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Five receivers at different depths and widths: flat (0 yards), short out (5), hook/sit (8), short out opposite (5), flat opposite (0).

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the underneath defenders and throw to the open window. Scan middle, then strong, then flat. Wherever the void is, the ball goes there.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Any zone (especially Cover 3 and Cover 4). Worst against press man, where defenders travel with receivers and there are no gaps to sit in.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The Cover 2 killer. A high-low read on the cornerback: a hitch underneath and a corner route over the top. The corner cannot cover both.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Outside receiver runs a 5-yard hitch. Inside receiver or slot runs a corner route to 12 to 15 yards.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the cornerback. IF he sits on the hitch, throw the corner over his head and under the safety. IF he bails to the corner, throw the hitch underneath.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 2 (the corner route attacks the exact hole Cover 2 cannot protect). Also good against Cover 4 (throw the hitch) and Cover 6 to the Cover 2 side. Risky against Cover 1 press.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The most coached concept in football. The same flat-defender read as Stick, but deeper, more yards at more risk.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Outside receiver runs a 12-yard curl (drive to 12, turn back). A back, slot, or tight end runs the flat. Backside receivers clear.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the flat defender. IF he widens to the flat, throw the curl. IF he sits on the curl, throw the flat.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3 (the curl sits in the hole between hook and deep third) and Cover 4. Worst against heavy underneath blitz.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The best man-coverage beater in football outside the deep ball. Two shallow crossers cross at the mesh point, creating natural picks that man defenders cannot navigate.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Two receivers run shallow crossing routes at 5 to 6 yards, crossing paths (the mesh point). Additional receivers sit or run wheels behind them at 10 to 15 yards.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Against man, throw to whichever crosser comes open first off the pick. Against zone, read the underneath defenders and sit a crosser in the void.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Man coverage (Cover 1, Cover 0, Cover 2 Man). Call it the moment the opponent switches to man to stop your RPOs. Aggressive zone that jumps the crosser can disrupt it.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The primary Cover 3 killer. A high-low read on the deep middle defender with a deep dig and a post behind it.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: One receiver runs a vertical to clear the safety. The outside receiver runs a deep dig at 15 to 18 yards behind the cleared zone.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the deep middle safety. IF he jumps the dig, throw the post behind him. IF he stays deep on the post, throw the dig underneath.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3 (the single high safety cannot cover the dig and the post). Also kills Tampa 2 (the dig sits where the Mike vacated).

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Four receivers run vertical routes that split and stress the deep zones. A universal vertical-stretch concept read off the safety count.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: Four vertical/seam routes. Outside receivers can convert to comebacks if covered deep.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the safety count. 1 high (MOFC): throw the seam away from the single safety. 2 high (MOFO): throw the seam between the two safeties. Against man: best matchup wins.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3 single deep safety, Cover 2 (the middle seam), and any shell that gets out-leveraged deep. Cover 3 Match and Cover 4 carry the verticals and defend it best.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: A deep two-man combination that attacks the deep middle and the sideline at the same time. A Cover 2 deep killer.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: A post route works inside to pull the deep safety. A wheel route runs behind him up the sideline.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the deep-half safety. IF he jumps the post, throw the wheel up the sideline. IF he stays on the wheel, throw the post into the vacated middle.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 2 and Cover 2 Man (attacks the deep-middle hole and the sideline). Good against Cover 1 (multiple deep options vs single high).

name: Flood (Three Levels)
subtitle: Intermediate · Vertical stretch of a zone

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Sends three receivers to the same side at three different depths (flat, intermediate, deep). Two zone defenders cannot cover three levels. Often run off a play-action boot.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: A deep route (corner or go), an intermediate route (out or dig at 12 to 15), and a flat route, all to one side.

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the deep-to-short defenders on that side. Throw to the level the defense does not cover, working high to low.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3 and zone coverages (overloads the underneath and deep zones to one side). Pairs with PA Boot.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: An RPO built on Inside Zone. The line run-blocks while the QB reads one defender to decide hand off or throw. The blocking looks identical whether it is a run or a quick pass.

sections.heading: Routes
sections.body: The line blocks Inside Zone. The slot runs a 5-yard hitch (the stick tag).

sections.heading: The read (IF-THEN)
sections.body: Read the conflict defender (backside or flat linebacker). IF he steps toward the run, pull and throw the stick behind him. IF he drops into coverage, hand off Inside Zone.

sections.heading: Beats
sections.body: Cover 3, the linebacker cannot fill the run and cover the stick. Note the illegal-man-downfield rule: the QB must decide in about 1 second.
